content.text: The workflow isn't in `tuist3` - the CI run is from `tuist/tuist` which is a different repo. Let me check the actual failed workflow from GitHub directly.

content.content:
mise WARN  missing: tuist@4.155.1
.github/workflows/docs.yml

input.command: mise exec gh -- gh api repos/tuist/tuist/contents/.github/workflows/docs.yml --jq '.content' | base64 -d
input.description: Get the docs workflow content from GitHub

content.content:
mise WARN  missing: tuist@4.155.1
name: Documentation
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
    paths:
      - docs/**
      - .github/workflows/docs.yml
      - cli/Sources/**
      - Package.swift
      - Package.resolved
      - mise/tasks/docs/**
      - docs/package.json
      - handbook/package.json
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, ready_for_review]
    paths:
      - docs/**
      - .github/workflows/docs.yml
      - cli/Sources/**
      - Package.swift
      - Package.resolved
      - mise/tasks/docs/**
      - docs/package.json
      - handbook/package.json

permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read

env:
  GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
  PNPM_HOME: ~/.pnpm
  TUIST_ENABLE_CACHING: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.fork != true }}

concurrency:
  group: docs-${{ github.head_ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  test-docker-compose:
    name: Test Docker Compose
    runs-on: namespace-profile-default
    timeout-minutes: 15
    if: github.event.pull_request.draft == false || github.event_name != 'pull_request'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Test docker-compose configuration
        run: ./docs/test-docker-compose.sh

  generate-cli-docs:
    name: Generate CLI Docs
    runs-on: macos-26
    timeout-minutes: 80
    if: github.event.pull_request.head.repo.fork != true && (github.event.pull_request.draft == false || github.event_name != 'pull_request')
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Restore PNPM Cache
        uses: actions/cache@v3
        id: pnpm-cache
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.pnpm/store
          key: pnpm-${{ hashFiles('pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
      - name: Restore docs node_modules cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        id: docs-node-modules-cache
        with:
          path: docs/node_modules
          key: docs-node-modules-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('docs/package.json') }}
      - name: Select Xcode
        run: sudo xcode-select -switch /Applications/Xcode_$(cat .xcode-version).app
      - name: Restore cache
        id: cache-restore
        uses: actions/cache/restore@v4
        with:
          path: .build
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('.xcode-version') }}-docs-${{ hashFiles('./Package.resolved') }}
          restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('.xcode-version') }}-docs-
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
        with:
          working_directory: docs
      - name: Authenticate with Tuist
        run: tuist auth login
      - name: Setup Tuist cache
        run: tuist setup cache
      - name: Install Tuist dependencies
        if: steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: tuist install
      - name: Generate CLI docs
        working-directory: docs
        run: mise run generate-cli-docs
      - name: Upload generated docs
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: docs-generated
          path: docs/docs/generated
      - name: Save cache
        id: cache-save
        uses: actions/cache/save@v4
        with:
          path: .build
          key: ${{ steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-primary-key }}

  build-deploy:
    name: Build and deploy docs
    runs-on: namespace-profile-default
    timeout-minutes: 80
    needs: [generate-cli-docs]
    if: github.event.pull_request.head.repo.fork != true && (github.event.pull_request.draft == false || github.event_name != 'pull_request')
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Download generated docs
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: docs-generated
          path: docs/docs/generated
      - name: Restore PNPM Cache
        uses: actions/cache@v3
        id: pnpm-cache
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.pnpm/store
          key: pnpm-${{ hashFiles('pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
      - name: Restore docs node_modules cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        id: docs-node-modules-cache
        with:
          path: docs/node_modules
          key: docs-node-modules-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('docs/package.json') }}
      - name: Restore VitePress cache
        uses: actions/cache/restore@v4
        id: vitepress-cache
        with:
          path: docs/.vitepress/cache
          key: vitepress-cache-${{ hashFiles('docs/package.json') }}
          restore-keys: vitepress-cache-
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
        with:
          working_directory: docs
      - name: Lint localization
        working-directory: docs
        run: mise run lint-localization
      - name: Free space
        # Our build process takes a lot of disk space, hitting limits on GitHub Actions runners.
        # This step ensures that we have enough space to build the documentation.
        # Reference: https://github.com/actions/runner-images/issues/2840?ref=dzombak.com#issuecomment-790492173
        run: |
          sudo rm -rf /usr/share/dotnet
          sudo rm -rf /opt/ghc
          sudo rm -rf "/usr/local/share/boost"
          sudo rm -rf "$AGENT_TOOLSDIRECTORY"
      - name: Build the documentation
        # VitePress is not memory-efficient, loading all the pages into memory when building
        # the site. This can potentially lead to out-of-memory errors in localized documentation
        # sites where the number of pages is multiplied by the number of languages.
        # For PRs, we only build English to speed up CI. All locales are built on main.
        env:
          NODE_OPTIONS: --max-old-space-size=12288
          DOCS_LOCALES: ${{ github.ref != 'refs/heads/main' && 'en' || '' }}
        working-directory: docs
        run: mise run build
      - name: Save VitePress cache
        if: ${{ hashFiles('docs/.vitepress/cache/**') != '' }}
        uses: actions/cache/save@v4
        with:
          path: docs/.vitepress/cache
          key: ${{ steps.vitepress-cache.outputs.cache-primary-key }}
      - uses: 1password/install-cli-action@v2
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
      - name: Deploy to Cloudflare Pages
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        working-directory: docs
        env:
          OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN }}
          CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: op:REDACTED
          CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID: op://tuist/Cloudflare/CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
        run: |
          op run -- wrangler pages deploy --commit-hash ${{ github.sha }} --branch ${{ github.ref_name }} --project-name tuist-docs .vitepress/dist

  build-deploy-project-description:
    name: Build and deploy ProjectDescription docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: swift:6.2
    timeout-minutes: 30
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Restore SPM cache
        id: spm-cache
        uses: actions/cache/restore@v4
        with:
          path: .build
          key: linux-docc-${{ hashFiles('Package.resolved') }}
          restore-keys: linux-docc-
      - name: Build DocC documentation
        run: |
          swift package --replace-scm-with-registry \
            --allow-writing-to-directory .build/documentation \
            generate-documentation \
            --product ProjectDescription \
            --disable-indexing \
            --output-path .build/documentation \
            --transform-for-static-hosting \
            --hosting-base-path ""
          echo "/ /documentation/projectdescription/project 301" > .build/documentation/_redirects
      - name: Save SPM cache
        uses: actions/cache/save@v4
        with:
          path: .build
          key: ${{ steps.spm-cache.outputs.cache-primary-key }}
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
      - name: Deploy to Cloudflare Pages
        env:
          OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN }}
          CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: op:REDACTED
          CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID: op://tuist/Cloudflare/CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
        run: |
          op run -- npx wrangler pages deploy --commit-hash ${{ github.sha }} --branch main --project-name tuist-projectdescription .build/documentation

        uses: actions/cache@v4
        id: docs-node-modules-cache
        with:
          path: docs/node_modules
          key: docs-node-modules-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('docs/package.json') }}
      - name: Select Xcode
        run: sudo xcode-select -switch /Applications/Xcode_$(cat .xcode-version).app
      - name: Restore cache
        id: cache-restore
        uses: actions/cache/restore@v4
        with:
          path: .build
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('.xcode-version') }}-docs-${{ hashFiles('./Package.resolved') }}
          restore-keys: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('.xcode-version') }}-docs-
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
        with:
          working_directory: docs
      - name: Authenticate with Tuist
        run: tuist auth login
      - name: Setup Tuist cache
        run: tuist setup cache
      - name: Install Tuist dependencies
        if: steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: tuist install
      - name: Generate CLI docs
        working-directory: docs
        run: mise run generate-cli-docs
      - name: Upload generated docs
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: docs-generated
          path: docs/docs/generated
      - name: Save cache
        id: cache-save
        uses: actions/cache/save@v4
        with:
          path: .build
          key: ${{ steps.cache-restore.outputs.cache-primary-key }}

  build-deploy:
    name: Build and deploy docs
    runs-on: namespace-profile-default
    timeout-minutes: 80
    needs: [generate-cli-docs]
    if: github.event.pull_request.head.repo.fork != true && (github.event.pull_request.draft == false || github.event_name != 'pull_request')
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Download generated docs
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: docs-generated
          path: docs/docs/generated
      - name: Restore PNPM Cache
        uses: actions/cache@v3
        id: pnpm-cache
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.pnpm/store
          key: pnpm-${{ hashFiles('pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
      - name: Restore docs node_modules cache
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        id: docs-node-modules-cache
        with:
          path: docs/node_modules
          key: docs-node-modules-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('docs/package.json') }}
      - name: Restore VitePress cache
        uses: actions/cache/restore@v4
        id: vitepress-cache
        with:
          path: docs/.vitepress/cache
          key: vitepress-cache-${{ hashFiles('docs/package.json') }}
          restore-keys: vitepress-cache-
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
        with:
          working_directory: docs
      - name: Lint localization
        working-directory: docs
        run: mise run lint-localization
      - name: Free space
        # Our build process takes a lot of disk space, hitting limits on GitHub Actions runners.
        # This step ensures that we have enough space to build the documentation.
        # Reference: https://github.com/actions/runner-images/issues/2840?ref=dzombak.com#issuecomment-790492173
        run: |
          sudo rm -rf /usr/share/dotnet
          sudo rm -rf /opt/ghc
          sudo rm -rf "/usr/local/share/boost"
          sudo rm -rf "$AGENT_TOOLSDIRECTORY"
      - name: Build the documentation
        # VitePress is not memory-efficient, loading all the pages into memory when building
        # the site. This can potentially lead to out-of-memory errors in localized documentation
        # sites where the number of pages is multiplied by the number of languages.
        # For PRs, we only build English to speed up CI. All locales are built on main.
        env:
          NODE_OPTIONS: --max-old-space-size=12288
          DOCS_LOCALES: ${{ github.ref != 'refs/heads/main' && 'en' || '' }}
        working-directory: docs
        run: mise run build
      - name: Save VitePress cache
        if: ${{ hashFiles('docs/.vitepress/cache/**') != '' }}
        uses: actions/cache/save@v4
        with:
          path: docs/.vitepress/cache
          key: ${{ steps.vitepress-cache.outputs.cache-primary-key }}
      - uses: 1password/install-cli-action@v2
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
      - name: Deploy to Cloudflare Pages
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        working-directory: docs
        env:
          OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN }}
          CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: op:REDACTED
          CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID: op://tuist/Cloudflare/CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
        run: |
          op run -- wrangler pages deploy --commit-hash ${{ github.sha }} --branch ${{ github.ref_name }} --project-name tuist-docs .vitepress/dist

  build-deploy-project-description:
    name: Build and deploy ProjectDescription docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    container:
      image: swift:6.2
    timeout-minutes: 30
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Restore SPM cache
        id: spm-cache
        uses: actions/cache/restore@v4
        with:
          path: .build
          key: linux-docc-${{ hashFiles('Package.resolved') }}
          restore-keys: linux-docc-
      - name: Build DocC documentation
        run: |
          swift package --replace-scm-with-registry \
            --allow-writing-to-directory .build/documentation \
            generate-documentation \
            --product ProjectDescription \
            --disable-indexing \
            --output-path .build/documentation \
            --transform-for-static-hosting \
            --hosting-base-path ""
          echo "/ /documentation/projectdescription/project 301" > .build/documentation/_redirects
      - name: Save SPM cache
        uses: actions/cache/save@v4
        with:
          path: .build
          key: ${{ steps.spm-cache.outputs.cache-primary-key }}
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
      - name: Deploy to Cloudflare Pages
        env:
          OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN }}
          CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: op:REDACTED
          CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID: op://tuist/Cloudflare/CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
        run: |
          op run -- npx wrangler pages deploy --commit-hash ${{ github.sha }} --branch main --project-name tuist-projectdescription .build/documentation

Found it. `ProjectDescription` is only added as a product inside `#if os(macOS)`, but the CI job runs on `ubuntu-latest` with `swift:6.2` container. That's why it's not found.

The fix is to move the `ProjectDescription` library product outside the `#if os(macOS)` block, or change the CI to build on macOS. Since DocC generation doesn't need macOS-specific features and `ProjectDescription` is just a library of Swift types, the simplest fix is to make it available on Linux too.

Let me check the `ProjectDescription` target to see if it has any macOS dependencies.

fix(cli): expose ProjectDescription product on Linux for DocC generation

The ProjectDescription library product was only declared inside an
`#if os(macOS)` block, causing the DocC documentation CI job (which
runs on Linux with swift:6.2) to fail with "no product named
ProjectDescription". This moves the product declaration outside the
macOS gate, keeping it dynamic on macOS and static on Linux.
EOF
)"

input.command:
mise exec gh -- gh pr create --title "fix(cli): expose ProjectDescription product on Linux for DocC generation" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
The `ProjectDescription` library product was only declared inside an `#if os(macOS)` block in `Package.swift`. The DocC documentation CI job runs on Linux (`swift:6.2` container), so `swift package generate-documentation --product ProjectDescription` was failing with "no product named ProjectDescription".

This moves the `ProjectDescription` product declaration outside the macOS-only gate, keeping it as a dynamic library on macOS (needed for manifest runtime loading) and a regular library on Linux (sufficient for DocC generation).
EOF
)"

content.text:
PR created: https://github.com/tuist/tuist/pull/9745

The issue was that `ProjectDescription` was declared as a product only inside `#if os(macOS)`, but the DocC CI job runs on Linux (`swift:6.2` container). The fix moves the product declaration outside that gate -- dynamic on macOS (needed for runtime manifest loading), regular/static on Linux (sufficient for DocC).

content.text: Pushed. The `ProjectDescription` target was also inside `#if os(macOS)`, so the product was referencing a target that didn't exist on Linux. Moved both the target (zero dependencies, compiles fine on Linux) and the product outside the macOS gate. CI should pass now.
